step_by_step_reasoning: We identify Edge Nodes (N) and Supernodes (SN) as Edge devices. EKS hosts the Mobile Discovery Service (MDS). MDS uses MongoDB for proximity clustering and DynamoDB for profile and association services. Supernodes send telemetry directly to Kinesis Data Streams, which feeds into Firehose, archives in S3, and is queried by Snowflake.

graph.name: Mimic Edge Engine Architecture
graph.notes: Edge Engine allows microservices to run directly on Android/iOS devices. Supernodes coordinate discovery and local clustering while forwarding logs to AWS.

nodes.name: Edge Node
nodes.notes: Standard mobile/IoT edge device running Edge Engine.

nodes.name: Supernode
nodes.notes: Coordinates local network node registrations and reports telemetry.

nodes.name: MongoDB
nodes.notes: Document DB used to define clusters based on physical proximity.

nodes.name: DynamoDB
nodes.notes: Stores profile data and hierarchical peer associations.

nodes.name: Kinesis Data Streams
nodes.notes: Ingests real-time logs and business logic metrics. Handles ~1000 msg/sec.

nodes.name: Snowflake
nodes.notes: Performs SQL queries over archived data for analytics.

edges.notes: Supernode registers the new node in MDS.
Sends API logs and business metrics.
